steps.title: Identify the right angle and complementary angles
steps.content:
Triangle RST has a right angle at S (marked with a square), with RS = 12 and ST = 5. Point W lies on $\overline{RT}$, so the rays $\overrightarrow{SR}$ and $\overrightarrow{ST}$ form the angle at S. The line segment $\overline{SW}$ divides this right angle into two parts: $\angle RSW$ and $\angle WST$.

Therefore:
$$\angle RSW + \angle WST = 90^{\circ}$$

steps.title: Use the complementary angle relationship
steps.content:
Since $\angle WST$ and $\angle RSW$ are complementary, we can write $\angle WST = 90^{\circ} - \angle RSW$. Apply the cofunction identity for sine and cosine:
$$\sin(\angle WST) = \sin(90^{\circ} - \angle RSW) = \cos(\angle RSW)$$

steps.title: Evaluate the expression
steps.content:
Substitute the identity into the given expression:
$$\cos(\angle RSW) - \sin(\angle WST) = \cos(\angle RSW) - \cos(\angle RSW) = 0$$
The answer is 0.
